channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Cointelegraph - Security, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading, Blockthreat, CryptoPotato, CryptoSlate, Krebs On Security, The Hacker News, Coindesk, GroupIB Blog, Data Breach Today, CNN.com - Crypto News, DataBreaches.net

item.title: Ethereum usage is at record highs yet ETH nears its longest monthly losing streak since 2018
item.description: Ethereum is approaching a milestone that few investors would welcome: its longest run of consecutive monthly losses since the 2018 crypto winter. Since September 2025, ETH has posted six straight mont...
item.dc:creator: CryptoSlate

item.dc:creator: DataBreaches.net

item.description: North Korean hackers are deploying newly uncovered tools to move data between internet-connected and air-gapped systems, spread via removable drives, and conduct covert surveillance. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Europol-led crackdown on The Com hackers leads to 30 arrests
item.description: A yearlong Europol-coordinated operation dubbed "Project Compass" has led to 30 arrests and 179 suspects being tied to "The Com," an online cybercrime collective that targets children and teenagers. [...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: DoJ Seizes $61 Million in Tether Linked to Pig Butchering Crypto Scams
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) this week announced the seizure of $61 million worth of Tether that were allegedly associated with bogus cryptocurrency schemes known as pig butchering. The confis...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: 900+ Sangoma FreePBX Instances Compromised in Ongoing Web Shell Attacks
item.description: The Shadowserver Foundation has revealed that over 900 Sangoma FreePBX instances still remain infected with web shells as part of attacks that exploited a command injection vulnerability starting in D...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: The Case for Why Better Breach Transparency Matters
item.description: It's become a standard practice for organizations to disclose the bare minimum about a data breach, or worse — not disclose the incident at all....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: CISA warns that RESURGE malware can be dormant on Ivanti devices
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has released new details about RESURGE, a malicious implant used in zero-day attacks exploiting CVE-2025-0282 to breach Ivanti Connect ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Malicious Go Crypto Module Steals Passwords, Deploys Rekoobe Backdoor
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a malicious Go module that's designed to harvest passwords, create persistent access via SSH, and deliver a Linux backdoor named Rekoobe. The Go mod...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Third-Party Patching and the Business Footprint We All Share
item.description: Everyday tools like PDF readers, email clients, and archive utilities quietly define the real attack surface. Action1 explains how third-party software drift increases exploit risk and why consistent ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Claude Code Security Shows Promise, Not Perfection
item.description: Claude Code's introduction rippled across the stock market, but researchers and analysts say its impact was overstated, as they peel back the layers....

item.title: Trojanized Gaming Tools Spread Java-Based RAT via Browser and Chat Platforms
item.description: Threat actors are luring unsuspecting users into running trojanized gaming utilities that are distributed via browsers and chat platforms to distribute a remote access trojan (RAT). "A malicious downl...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Meta Files Lawsuits Against Brazil, China, Vietnam Advertisers Over Celeb-Bait Scams
item.description: Meta on Thursday said it's taking legal action to tackle scams on its platforms by filing lawsuits against what it calls deceptive advertisers based in Brazil, China, and Vietnam. As part of the effor...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Marquis v. SonicWall Lawsuit Ups the Breach Blame Game
item.description: When a company gets breached through a third-party security vendor, who should bear responsibility? For one FinTech company, the answer is the firewall provider....

item.title: Cisco SD-WAN Zero-Day Under Exploitation for 3 Years
item.description: The maximum-severity vulnerability CVE-2026-20127 was exploited by an unknown but sophisticated threat actor who left very little evidence behind....
